description:
You are an on–call operations engineer who has just received an alert that service A
    is under heavy load.  A temporary mitigation must be applied in the “infra-config”
    Git repository that lives in your home directory.

    STARTING POINT
      • A local Git repository already exists at
        /home/user/infra-config
      • The repository is on branch “main” and contains a single
        tracked file called
        /home/user/infra-config/config/serviceA.yml
      • The current content of that file is **exactly**

            service_name: serviceA
            replicas: 3
            image: serviceA:1.2.0

    WHAT YOU MUST DO
      1. Create and check out a new branch named
         hotfix/incident-23145
      2. Edit config/serviceA.yml so that the line
         replicas: 3
         becomes
         replicas: 4
      3. Commit the change with the commit message
         Increase replicas for serviceA to mitigate incident #23145
         (use this message verbatim; do not add extra lines)
      4. Add an **annotated** tag that points to the new commit.
         The tag must be named
             incident-23145-mitigation
         and the tag annotation text must be
             tag for incident 23145 replica increase
      5. Switch back to branch “main” and merge the
         hotfix/incident-23145 branch using a **no-fast-forward merge**
         so that the merge itself produces a new commit.
      6. Create the following plain-text incident report file
         /home/user/incidents/incident-23145.log
         If the directory /home/user/incidents does not exist, create it.
         The file must follow this 5-line format *exactly*:

            INCIDENT_ID: 23145
            BRANCH: hotfix/incident-23145
            TAG: incident-23145-mitigation
            STATUS: Mitigated
            FILE_UPDATED: config/serviceA.yml

         • Each item must appear on its own line.
         • There must be **exactly** 5 lines and **no** blank line at the end of the file.
      7. After completing all steps, you should still be on branch “main”.
         A quick `git log --oneline --decorate -3` should show the merge
         commit, followed by the tagged hot-fix commit, followed by the
         original commit that was present at the start of the exercise.

    SUCCESS CRITERIA (checked by automated tests)
      • /home/user/infra-config/config/serviceA.yml exists and the
        value “replicas” is now 4 (no other lines changed).
      • The branch “hotfix/incident-23145” still exists and its HEAD commit
        message is exactly the one specified in step 3.
      • The annotated tag “incident-23145-mitigation” exists and
        points to that same commit.
      • Branch “main” contains a merge commit whose parents are
        (a) the previous main-branch commit and (b) the hot-fix commit.
      • /home/user/incidents/incident-23145.log exists and
        matches the 5-line format shown above *exactly*.
      • Your working directory is clean (`git status` shows no
        untracked or modified files) and you are currently on branch “main”.
truth:
INITIAL_REPO_PATH: /home/user/infra-config
    INITIAL_FILE_PATH: /home/user/infra-config/config/serviceA.yml
    INITIAL_FILE_CONTENTS:
        service_name: serviceA
        replicas: 3
        image: serviceA:1.2.0

    EXPECTED_FILE_CONTENTS_AFTER:
        service_name: serviceA
        replicas: 4
        image: serviceA:1.2.0

    INCIDENT_LOG_PATH: /home/user/incidents/incident-23145.log
    INCIDENT_LOG_CONTENTS:
        INCIDENT_ID: 23145
        BRANCH: hotfix/incident-23145
        TAG: incident-23145-mitigation
        STATUS: Mitigated
        FILE_UPDATED: config/serviceA.yml

    MERGE_COMMIT_REQUIREMENTS:
        • Must be on branch main.
        • Must have two parents.
        • Parent1 = previous main commit (hash of initial repo state).
        • Parent2 = commit tagged incident-23145-mitigation.

    TAG_NAME: incident-23145-mitigation
    TAG_ANNOTATION: tag for incident 23145 replica increase
    HOTFIX_BRANCH_NAME: hotfix/incident-23145
    HOTFIX_COMMIT_MSG: Increase replicas for serviceA to mitigate incident #23145
name: task_000000_3c135ad7
